 005: 
 006: """
 007: HLL Core Operations in Cython — Bitmap Register Model
 008: 
 009: Faithful port of the original HllSets.jl Julia implementation.
 010: 
 011: Each register is a uint32 bitmap where bit k is set when an element
 012: with k trailing zeros is hashed to that bucket. This preserves the
 013: full set of observations, enabling TRUE bitwise set operations:
 014: 
 015:   Union:        OR  (A | B)      — elements in either set
 016:   Intersection: AND (A & B)      — elements in both sets
 017:   Difference:   AND-NOT (A & ~B) — elements in A but not B
 018:   XOR:          XOR (A ^ B)      — elements in exactly one set
 019: 
 020: All operations return new HLLCore instances (immutability).
 021: 
 022: Cardinality is computed via standard HLL algorithm using highest_set_bit
 023: (equivalent to Julia's maxidx) per register → harmonic mean + corrections.
 024: 
 025: Serialization: Registers flattened to bit positions → Roaring bitmap.
 026:   bit_position = register_index * 32 + bit_index
 027:   Prefixed with b'HLL2' for format identification.
 028:   Backward-compatible: reads old uint8 format (position*256 + value).
 029: """
 030:

 186:         Internal: hash token, compute bucket and trailing zeros,
 187:         set corresponding bit in the register bitmap.

 190:             h = u_hash(x; seed=seed)

PyDoc_STRVAR(__pyx_doc_4core_8hll_core_7HLLCore_30get_registers_roaring, "\n        Serialize registers as Roaring bitmap of bit positions.\n\n        Flattens the 2^P \303\227 32 bitmap tensor to 1D bit positions:\n            For register i, if bit j is set \342\206\222 position = i * 32 + j\n\n        This matches Julia's to_binary_tensor() \342\206\222 flatten_tensor() approach,\n        but compressed with Roaring bitmap.\n\n        Prefixed with b'HLL2' for format identification.\n        ");

 551:         """
 552:         Serialize registers as Roaring bitmap of bit positions.
 553: 
 554:         Flattens the 2^P × 32 bitmap tensor to 1D bit positions:
 555:             For register i, if bit j is set → position = i * 32 + j
 556: 
 557:         This matches Julia's to_binary_tensor() → flatten_tensor() approach,
 558:         but compressed with Roaring bitmap.
 559: 
 560:         Prefixed with b'HLL2' for format identification.
 561:         """

+583:     def set_registers_roaring(self, bytes data):
/* Python wrapper */
static PyObject *__pyx_pw_4core_8hll_core_7HLLCore_33set_registers_roaring(PyObject *__pyx_v_self, 
#if CYTHON_METH_FASTCALL

#endif
); /*proto*/
PyDoc_STRVAR(__pyx_doc_4core_8hll_core_7HLLCore_32set_registers_roaring, "\n        Deserialize registers from Roaring bitmap.\n\n        Auto-detects format:\n          - HLL2 (new): b'HLL2' prefix, bit-position encoding\n          - Legacy (old): No prefix, position*256+value encoding\n\n        Legacy data is converted to bitmap model by setting the\n        highest bit corresponding to the old register value.\n        Cardinality is preserved; intersection of legacy + new is\n        NOT meaningful (different hash models).\n        ");

 584:         """
 585:         Deserialize registers from Roaring bitmap.
 586: 
 587:         Auto-detects format:
 588:           - HLL2 (new): b'HLL2' prefix, bit-position encoding
 589:           - Legacy (old): No prefix, position*256+value encoding
 590: 
 591:         Legacy data is converted to bitmap model by setting the
 592:         highest bit corresponding to the old register value.
 593:         Cardinality is preserved; intersection of legacy + new is
 594:         NOT meaningful (different hash models).
